name: Aruba

colors.symbolism: the sky and the Caribbean Sea surrounding Aruba
abundance, the sun, the gold-mining past, and the wanglo (Brazilwood) flowers
love for the country, the brazilwood from which the island took its early economy, and the Indian heritage
honesty and the white sand beaches; the fimbriation around the star

description: A light-blue field with two narrow yellow horizontal stripes near the bottom, and a red four-pointed star fimbriated in white in the upper hoist.
history: Adopted on 18 March 1976, four years before Aruba was granted Status Aparte (separate constituent country status) within the Kingdom of the Netherlands on 1 January 1986. The flag was designed by a commission led by J. M. (Pancho) Geerman; 18 March is now celebrated as National Anthem and Flag Day.

features.symbolism: the two narrow yellow stripes near the bottom evoke the abundance of the island and its position as separate from the other Netherlands Antilles

the four cardinal points referring to the many origins of Aruba's population; the four main languages (Papiamento, Spanish, Dutch, English); the red colour symbolises the brazilwood, love for the country, and the blood of patriots
